revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. {{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Medieval women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:kk]] She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Medieval women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:1333 deaths]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Medieval women poets]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Japanese women poets]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[category:1 revision imported]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]]{{Japan-royal-stub}}

Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Saionji {{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq 2]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Saionji Kishi]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Saionji Kishi}} [[Category:Pages Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[de jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[de facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]]

She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[Japanese empresses|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[Oki Islands]] by the [[Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]
