Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

revision.text:
{{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[sn:Saionji Kishi]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}}

{{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|qid=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|qid=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|qid=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|qid=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|qid=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|qid=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|qid=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|qid=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|qid=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}}

Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}}

revision.text:
[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}}

[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = [[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|[[:en:Saionji|Saionji]] (by birth)|[[:en:Imperial House of Japan|Imperial House of Japan]] (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]] of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''[[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka]]'' poetry are included in ''[[:en:Chokusen wakashū|Chokusen wakashū]]'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She [[:en:elopement|eloped]] with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of [[:en:Emperor Go-Murakami|Emperor Go-Murakami]]), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by [[:en:Bai Juyi|Bai Juyi]], and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''[[:en:Masukagami|Masukagami]]'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the [[:en:Oki Islands|Oki Islands]] by the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura shogunate]] in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''[[:en:De jure|De jure]]'' "Empress Dowager", but ''[[:en:De facto|De facto]]'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to [[:en:Emperor Kōgon|Emperor Kōgon]] == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= [[:en:Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|Princess Shōshi]] | title=[[:en:Empress of Japan|Empress consort of Japan]] | after=[[:en:Princess Junshi|Princess Junshi]] | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|{{ill|Saionji|en|Saionji|WD=Q428664|lt=Saionji}} (by birth)|{{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka}}'' poetry are included in ''{{ill|Chokusen wakashū|en|Chokusen wakashū|WD=Q1516890|lt=Chokusen wakashū}}'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She {{ill|elopement|en|elopement|WD=Q2812388|lt=eloped}} with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|en|Emperor Go-Murakami|WD=Q440447|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami}}), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by {{ill|Bai Juyi|en|Bai Juyi|WD=Q313324|lt=Bai Juyi}}, and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''{{ill|Masukagami|en|Masukagami|WD=Q3019579|lt=Masukagami}}'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the {{ill|Oki Islands|en|Oki Islands|WD=Q483146|lt=Oki Islands}} by the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''{{ill|De jure|en|De jure|WD=Q132555|lt=De jure}}'' "Empress Dowager", but ''{{ill|De facto|en|De facto|WD=Q712144|lt=De facto}}'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to {{ill|Emperor Kōgon|en|Emperor Kōgon|WD=Q471885|lt=Emperor Kōgon}} == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|en|Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|WD=Q8766149|lt=Princess Shōshi}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Princess Junshi|en|Princess Junshi|WD=Q7244659|lt=Princess Junshi}} | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] [[en:Saionji Kishi]][[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]][[fr:Saionji no Kishi]][[id:Saionji no Kishi]][[ja:西園寺禧子]][[ko:사이온지 기시]][[sv:Saionji no Kishi]][[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]][[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]][[vi:Saionji Kishi]][[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png|birth_date=1295; 1304|death_date={{death date|1333|11|19}}|birth_place={{ill|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}|title={{ill|Chūgū|WD=Q10875159}}; {{ill|empress Dowager|WD=Q7409248}}|spouse={{ill|Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942}}|children={{ill|Kanshi-naishinnō|WD=Q11495331}}|father={{ill|Saionji Sanekane|WD=Q7223333}}|mother={{ill|Q110256536|WD=Q110256536}}|family={{ill|Saionji family|WD=Q7402472}}|religion={{ill|Buddhism|WD=Q748}}|native_name=西園寺禧子|given_name={{ill|Sachiko|WD=Q1231005}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q7223538]] Wikidata name: Saionji Kishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Distinguish|Saionji Kisshi}} {{family name hatnote|Saionji|lang=Japanese}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|西園寺禧子|date=November 2011}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2021}} {{Infobox royalty |name = Saionji Kishi
{{lang|ja|西園寺禧子}} |title = {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|Kōtaigō-gū}} |image = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo.png |alt = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo |caption = Empress Kishi and Emperor Go-Daigo from ''Taiheiki Emaki'' ({{c.}}17th century) |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} |reign = 21 September 1319 – 19 November 1333 |consort = yes |spouse = {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} |issue = Princess Kanshi |house = {{unbulleted list|{{ill|Saionji|en|Saionji|WD=Q428664|lt=Saionji}} (by birth)|{{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|WD=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan}} (by marriage)}} |father = Saionji Sanekane |mother = Fujiwara no Takako (藤原孝子) |birth_date = after {{c.}}1295{{snd}}before 1305 |birth_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |death_date = 19 November 1333 (aged 27–{{c.}}38) |death_place = Heian-kyō (Kyōto) |date of burial = |place of burial = |regnal name = {{Nihongo|Go-Kyōgoku-in|後京極院}} |}} {{Nihongo|'''Saionji Kishi'''|西園寺 禧子||? – 19 November 1333}}, or more formally {{Nihongo|'''Fujiwara no Kishi'''|藤原 禧子}}, was an empress consort of Japan. She was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}} of Japan.Mori Shigeaki. Go-Daigo tennō: nanboku-chō dōran o irodotta haō (後醍醐天皇: 南北朝動乱を彩った覇王). Tokyo: Chūōkōronshinsha, 2000. {{ISBN|4-12-101521-5}} She was given the regnal name ({{Nihongo|join-gō|女院号}}) {{Nihongo|'''Reiseimon-in'''|礼成門院}} in 1332 when her husband was banished, but it was abolished when he returned to the [[:en:Chrysanthemum throne|Chrysanthemum throne]] in 1333. Later she was given the second regnal name {{Nihongo|'''Go-Kyōgoku-in'''|後京極院}} upon her death. She was also an excellent poet, 14 of whose ''{{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka}}'' poetry are included in ''{{ill|Chokusen wakashū|en|Chokusen wakashū|WD=Q1516890|lt=Chokusen wakashū}}'' (imperially-commissioned anthologies). == Biography == [[File:On the Lamentation of the Empress.png|thumb|left|Kishi lamented that her husband was sentenced to exile. She then rushed to his prison by ox-carriage under the cover of night and stayed with him until morning. From ''Taiheiki Emaki'' (c. 17th century), vol. 2, On the Lamentation of the Empress. Owned by [[Saitama Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore]].]] She was born as the 3rd daughter of Saionji Sanekane (西園寺実兼). She {{ill|elopement|en|elopement|WD=Q2812388|lt=eloped}} with then-Crown Prince Takaharu (later Emperor Go-Daigo) in 1313 and officially got married with him in 1314. Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|en|Emperor Go-Murakami|WD=Q440447|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami}}), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by {{ill|Bai Juyi|en|Bai Juyi|WD=Q313324|lt=Bai Juyi}}, and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''{{ill|Masukagami|en|Masukagami|WD=Q3019579|lt=Masukagami}}'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the {{ill|Oki Islands|en|Oki Islands|WD=Q483146|lt=Oki Islands}} by the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''{{ill|De jure|en|De jure|WD=Q132555|lt=De jure}}'' "Empress Dowager", but ''{{ill|De facto|en|De facto|WD=Q712144|lt=De facto}}'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to {{ill|Emperor Kōgon|en|Emperor Kōgon|WD=Q471885|lt=Emperor Kōgon}} == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|en|Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|WD=Q8766149|lt=Princess Shōshi}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Princess Junshi|en|Princess Junshi|WD=Q7244659|lt=Princess Junshi}} | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Prince Takaharu acceded to the throne as Emperor Go-Daigo in the 2nd lunar month, 1318 and Kishi was made {{Nihongo|semi-Empress consort|女御|nyogo}} in the 4th lunar month of the same year. She was made Empress consort (chūgū) in the 8th lunar month, 1319. Although vol. 1 of the historical epic ''[[Taiheiki]]'' tells she lost the emperor's favor because of her lady-in-waiting [[Ano Renshi]] (mother of {{ill|Emperor Go-Murakami|en|Emperor Go-Murakami|WD=Q440447|lt=Emperor Go-Murakami}}), Hiromi Hyodo, a Japanese literature researcher, claims that the story is the imitation of a poem by {{ill|Bai Juyi|en|Bai Juyi|WD=Q313324|lt=Bai Juyi}}, and in the real history Kishi and Go-Daigo were a close and affectionate couple. Other sources such as vol. 4 of the same epic (as later illustrated in ''Taiheiki Emaki'', vol. 2), ''{{ill|Masukagami|en|Masukagami|WD=Q3019579|lt=Masukagami}}'', several historical documents, and poetry by the couple's own hands, show the deep intimacy between the emperor and empress. Emperor Go-Daigo was captured and exiled to the {{ill|Oki Islands|en|Oki Islands|WD=Q483146|lt=Oki Islands}} by the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate}} in the 3rd lunar month, 1332 and Kishi became a Buddhist nun in the 8th month the same year. Emperor Go-Daigo escaped from the Oki Islands and returned to Kyoto in the 6th lunar month, 1333. After that, Kishi resumed the title of Empress consort (chūgū) and a little later was made {{Nihongo|High Empress|皇太后宮|kōtaigō-gū|''{{ill|De jure|en|De jure|WD=Q132555|lt=De jure}}'' "Empress Dowager", but ''{{ill|De facto|en|De facto|WD=Q712144|lt=De facto}}'' higher consort-title than "Empress" (''chūgū'')}}. She died on the 10th lunar month 12th, 1333. Issue: * princess (1314–?), died young * Imperial Princess ''Kanshi'' (懽子内親王) (Senseimon-in, 宣政門院) (1315–1362), [[Saiō]] at [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise Shrine]]; later, married to {{ill|Emperor Kōgon|en|Emperor Kōgon|WD=Q471885|lt=Emperor Kōgon}} == Notes == {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|en|Princess Shōshi (1286–1348)|WD=Q8766149|lt=Princess Shōshi}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan}} | after={{ill|Princess Junshi|en|Princess Junshi|WD=Q7244659|lt=Princess Junshi}} | years=1319–1333}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Empress dowagers of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} [[en:Saionji Kishi]] [[fa:فوجیوارا نو کیشی]] [[fr:Saionji no Kishi]] [[id:Saionji no Kishi]] [[ja:西園寺禧子]] [[ko:사이온지 기시]] [[sv:Saionji no Kishi]] [[th:ไซอนจิ คิชิ]] [[uk:Сайондзі Кісі]] [[vi:Saionji Kishi]] [[zh:西園寺禧子]] {{wikidata link|Q7223538}} [[Category:1333 deaths]] [[Category:14th-century Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Emperor Go-Daigo]] [[Category:Japanese Buddhist nuns]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese women poets]] [[Category:Nobility from Kyoto]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
